bonuses.leadin: A character with this name says, “Jesus Christ was a big man. He was the Son of the Father. I too. I am the son of my father” in a play where he cuts the protagonist’s throat. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: A character with this name says, "Jesus Christ was a big man. He was the Son of the Father. I too. I am the son of my father" in a play where he cuts the protagonist's throat. For 10 points each:
parts: Give this name of a character advised by the wise man Stool-Pigeon. An ex-con who is the second of this name titles a play where he sells stolen refrigerators to try to buy a video store.

King Hedley originally appeared in a play focusing on a man in this profession, Floyd “Schoolboy” Barton. In an earlier play, Levee and Slow Drag wait for a real-life woman in this profession named Ma Rainey.
Seven Guitars and Ma Rainey’s Black Bottom are part of this American author’s Pittsburgh Cycle.
parts_sanitized: Give this name of a character advised by the wise man Stool-Pigeon. An ex-con who is the second of this name titles a play where he sells stolen refrigerators to try to buy a video store.

answers: King Hedley [or King Hedley; accept King Hedley II or King Hedley II]
answers_sanitized: King Hedley [or King Hedley; accept King Hedley II or King Hedley II]
August Wilson [or Frederick August Kittel, Jr.]

bonuses.leadin: In one play, a character with this name murders Floyd Barton thinking that he’s the musician Buddy Bolden delivering money to him. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: In one play, a character with this name murders Floyd Barton thinking that he's the musician Buddy Bolden delivering money to him. For 10 points each:
parts: Give this name also shared by a man who sells stolen refrigerators in a play that ends with him being killed by his mother Ruby.
Those aforementioned plays, Seven Guitars and King Hedley II, are set in this city.
Seven Guitars and King Hedley II are two plays from this author’s 10-part Pittsburgh Cycle alongside Fences.
